peer_groups: 有色金属-锂资源与锂盐 | 有色金属-战略资源与矿业
watch_reasons: Accepted. Salt Lake is retained as the domestic salt-lake potassium and lithium anchor. Its moat comes from Qarhan salt-lake resource access; high margins; brine and mining-right constraints; and a role not covered by hard-rock lithium peers. | Retained. Salt Lake is not a global potash monopoly. It has a hard-to-replicate domestic strategic-resource position in Qarhan salt-lake potash and salt-lake lithium; very high 2025 margins; large mining-right area after resource integration; and capacity constrained by scarce brine resources and permits.
calibrated_standard_implications: Domestic salt-lake lithium and potassium platforms can qualify without global monopoly when resource access is scarce; margins are structurally supported; and the advantage is not mainly lithium-price beta. | A domestic monopoly or near-monopoly can qualify when the mineral is import-sensitive; resource access is scarce; margins are structurally high; and the advantage is not mainly commodity-price beta.

peer_groups: 有色金属-锂资源与锂盐

peer_groups: 有色金属-锂资源与锂盐
watch_reasons: Revised to watch after reviewer challenge. In a lithium-only comparison Zangge is weaker than Salt Lake; however at listed-company level it combines potash cash flow; salt-lake lithium optionality; a material Julong Copper stake whose 2025 investment income dominated profit; and Zijin's new controlling-shareholder role. This cross-resource package is hard to replicate and deserves observation while risks from copper price beta; equity-method earnings; and less direct operating control than Zijin must be tracked.
calibrated_standard_implications: A company rejected inside a narrow peer group must be rechecked for cross-industry scarce-resource assets or controlling-shareholder capability. Retain it if the aggregate listed-company thesis is material; hard to replicate; and not merely a weaker copy of a retained single-industry peer.

peer_groups: 有色金属-锂资源与锂盐
watch_reasons: Retained with lower conviction. peer_groups: 有色金属-战略资源与矿业
watch_reasons: Retained. Xiamen Tungsten is not a pure miner but tungsten is globally supply-concentrated and China applies mining quota controls. The company has an integrated tungsten-molybdenum chain; meaningful upstream self-supply; strong tungsten-molybdenum margins; and globally competitive molybdenum and tungsten material positions.
calibrated_standard_implications: For tungsten retain differentiated companies when resource constraint; quota control; high-value processing; and strong step-level margins are all present. Pure mining status is not required if the processing moat is resource-backed.

peer_groups: 有色金属-战略资源与矿业
watch_reasons: Retained. China Tungsten High-Tech has stronger company-level evidence than a normal material processor. Its annual report describes Shizhuyuan as the world's largest single operating tungsten mine and public disclosures identify its cemented-carbide scale as global No. 1. Margins and R&D intensity are acceptable for high-end tungsten materials.
calibrated_standard_implications: Keep both Xiamen Tungsten and China Tungsten High-Tech because they are differentiated. Xiamen Tungsten is an integrated tungsten-molybdenum and material platform while China Tungsten High-Tech is a hard-alloy tooling and Shizhuyuan-backed tungsten platform.
